sections.paragraph:
δυνατῶς adv. of δύνατος (Hdt. 7, 11; Michel 1001 VII, 27 [c. 200 b.c.]; ISardGauthier 4, 8; Plut.; 1 Ch 26:8; Wsd 6:6) strongly δ. ὁρᾶν see as keenly as possible Hs 9, 1, 3 (cp. Philo, Det. Pot. Ins. 130. Hermas’ use of the δυν-family is quite imagistic).—M-M. s.v. δυνατός.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Michel Michel = CM., Recueil d’inscriptions grecques—List 3

ISardGauthier ISardGauthier = Nouvelles inscriptions de Sardes II, ed. PGauthier—List 3

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Hermas Hermas , s. H—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 264
